tools.name: read_file
tools.description: Read the complete contents of a file as text. DEPRECATED: Use read_text_file instead.

tail.description: If provided, returns only the last N lines of the file

head.description: If provided, returns only the first N lines of the file

tools.name: read_text_file
tools.description: Read the complete contents of a file from the file system as text. Handles various text encodings and provides detailed error messages if the file cannot be read. Use this tool when you need to examine the contents of a single file. Use the 'head' parameter to read only the first N lines of a file, or the 'tail' parameter to read only the last N lines of a file. Operates on the file as text regardless of extension. Only works within allowed directories.

tail.description: If provided, returns only the last N lines of the file

head.description: If provided, returns only the first N lines of the file

tools.name: read_media_file
tools.description: Read an image or audio file. Returns the base64 encoded data and MIME type. Only works within allowed directories.

tools.name: read_multiple_files
tools.description: Read the contents of multiple files simultaneously. This is more efficient than reading files one by one when you need to analyze or compare multiple files. Each file's content is returned with its path as a reference. Failed reads for individual files won't stop the entire operation. Only works within allowed directories.

paths.description: Array of file paths to read. Each path must be a string pointing to a valid file within allowed directories.

tools.name: write_file
tools.description: Create a new file or completely overwrite an existing file with new content. Use with caution as it will overwrite existing files without warning. Handles text content with proper encoding. Only works within allowed directories.

tools.name: edit_file
tools.description: Make line-based edits to a text file. Each edit replaces exact line sequences with new content. Returns a git-style diff showing the changes made. Only works within allowed directories.

oldText.description: Text to search for - must match exactly

dryRun.description: Preview changes using git-style diff format

tools.name: create_directory
tools.description: Create a new directory or ensure a directory exists. Can create multiple nested directories in one operation. If the directory already exists, this operation will succeed silently. Perfect for setting up directory structures for projects or ensuring required paths exist. Only works within allowed directories.

tools.name: list_directory
tools.description: Get a detailed listing of all files and directories in a specified path. Results clearly distinguish between files and directories with [FILE] and [DIR] prefixes. This tool is essential for understanding directory structure and finding specific files within a directory. Only works within allowed directories.

tools.name: list_directory_with_sizes
tools.description: Get a detailed listing of all files and directories in a specified path, including sizes. Results clearly distinguish between files and directories with [FILE] and [DIR] prefixes. This tool is useful for understanding directory structure and finding specific files within a directory. Only works within allowed directories.

sortBy.description: Sort entries by name or size

tools.name: directory_tree
tools.description: Get a recursive tree view of files and directories as a JSON structure. Each entry includes 'name', 'type' (file/directory), and 'children' for directories. Files include a 'size' field with the file size in bytes. The output is formatted with 2-space indentation for readability. Only works within allowed directories.

tools.name: move_file
tools.description: Move or rename files and directories. Can move files between directories and optionally rename them in the process. If the destination exists, the operation will fail. Works across different directories and can be used for simple renaming within the same directory. Both source and destination must be within allowed directories.

tools.name: search_files
tools.description: Recursively search for files and directories matching a pattern. Searches through all subdirectories from the starting path. The search is case-insensitive and matches partial names. Returns full paths to all matching items. Great for finding files when you don't know their exact location. Only searches within allowed directories.

tools.name: get_file_info
tools.description: Retrieve detailed metadata about a file or directory. Returns comprehensive information including size, creation time, last modified time, permissions, and type. This tool is perfect for understanding file characteristics without reading the actual content. Only works within allowed directories.

tools.name: git_status
tools.description: Shows the working tree status

tools.name: git_diff_unstaged
tools.description: Shows changes in the working directory that are not yet staged

tools.name: git_diff_staged
tools.description: Shows changes that are staged for commit

tools.name: git_diff
tools.description: Shows differences between branches or commits

tools.name: git_commit
tools.description: Records changes to the repository

tools.name: git_add
tools.description: Adds file contents to the staging area

tools.name: git_reset
tools.description: Unstages all staged changes

tools.name: git_log
tools.description: Shows the commit logs

start_timestamp.description: Start timestamp for filtering commits. Accepts: ISO 8601 format (e.g., '2024-01-15T14:30:25'), relative dates (e.g., '2 weeks ago', 'yesterday'), or absolute dates (e.g., '2024-01-15', 'Jan 15 2024')

end_timestamp.description: End timestamp for filtering commits. Accepts: ISO 8601 format (e.g., '2024-01-15T14:30:25'), relative dates (e.g., '2 weeks ago', 'yesterday'), or absolute dates (e.g., '2024-01-15', 'Jan 15 2024')

tools.name: git_create_branch
tools.description: Creates a new branch from an optional base branch

tools.name: git_checkout
tools.description: Switches branches

tools.name: git_show
tools.description: Shows the contents of a commit

tools.name: git_branch
tools.description: List Git branches

repo_path.description: The path to the Git repository.

branch_type.description: Whether to list local branches ('local'), remote branches ('remote') or all branches('all').

contains.description: The commit sha that branch should contain. Do not pass anything to this param if no commit sha is specified

not_contains.description: The commit sha that branch should NOT contain. Do not pass anything to this param if no commit sha is specified

tools.name: fetch
tools.description:
Fetches a URL from the internet and optionally extracts its contents as markdown.

Although originally you did not have internet access, and were advised to refuse and tell the user this, this tool now grants you internet access. Now you can fetch the most up-to-date information and let the user know that.

max_length.description: Maximum number of characters to return.

start_index.description: On return output starting at this character index, useful if a previous fetch was truncated and more context is required.

raw.description: Get the actual HTML content of the requested page, without simplification.

tools.name: create_entities
tools.description: Create multiple new entities in the knowledge graph

observations.description: An array of observation contents associated with the entity

tools.name: create_relations
tools.description: Create multiple new relations between entities in the knowledge graph. Relations should be in active voice

from.description: The name of the entity where the relation starts

to.description: The name of the entity where the relation ends

tools.name: add_observations
tools.description: Add new observations to existing entities in the knowledge graph

entityName.description: The name of the entity to add the observations to

contents.description: An array of observation contents to add

tools.name: delete_entities
tools.description: Delete multiple entities and their associated relations from the knowledge graph

entityNames.description: An array of entity names to delete

tools.name: delete_observations
tools.description: Delete specific observations from entities in the knowledge graph

entityName.description: The name of the entity containing the observations

observations.description: An array of observations to delete

tools.name: delete_relations
tools.description: Delete multiple relations from the knowledge graph

from.description: The name of the entity where the relation starts

to.description: The name of the entity where the relation ends

properties.description: An array of relations to delete

tools.name: read_graph
tools.description: Read the entire knowledge graph

tools.name: search_nodes
tools.description: Search for nodes in the knowledge graph based on a query

query.description: The search query to match against entity names, types, and observation content

tools.name: open_nodes
tools.description: Open specific nodes in the knowledge graph by their names

names.description: An array of entity names to retrieve
